heading: Establishment
label: 2200g Establishment
notes: Amendments2024—Subsec. (a). substituted “Under Secretary of Defense” for “Under Secretary for Defense”.Pub. L. 118–1592023—Subsec. (a). inserted heading.Pub. L. 118–312022— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), substituted “The Secretary of Defense, acting through the Under Secretary for Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, shall establish and maintain” for “For the purposes of preparing selected students for public service in Department of Defense occupations relating to acquisition, science, engineering, or other civilian occupations determined by the Secretary of Defense, and to target critical skill gaps in the Department of Defense, the Secretary of Defense shall establish and maintain”, and added subsecs. (b) and (c).Pub. L. 117–2632021— substituted “§” for “SEC.” in section designation.Pub. L. 116–283

Implementation Timeline, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–92, div. A, title VIII, § 860(b)133 Stat. 1514Dec. 20, 2019“(1).—Initial implementationNot later than , the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the congressional defense committees [Committees on Armed Services and Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives] a plan and schedule to implement the Defense Civilian Training Corps program established under chapter 113 of title 10, United States Code (as added by subsection (a)) at one accredited civilian educational institution authorized to grant baccalaureate degrees not later than . The plan shall include a list of critical skills gaps the program will address and recommendations for any legislative changes required for effective implementation of the program.February 15, 2020August 1, 2021“(2).—ExpansionNot later than , the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the congressional defense committees an expansion plan and schedule to expand the Defense Civilian Training Corps program to five accredited civilian educational institutions not later than .December 31, 2020August 1, 2022“(3).—Full implementationNot later than , the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the congressional defense committees a full implementation plan and schedule to expand the Defense Civilian Training Corps program to at least 20 accredited civilian educational institutions with not fewer than 400 members enrolled in the program not later than .”December 31, 2021August 1, 2023

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary of Defense, acting through the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, shall establish and maintain a Defense Civilian Training Corps program, organized into one or more units, at any accredited civilian educational institution authorized to grant baccalaureate degrees.

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: b .—Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Defense Civilian Training Corps is to target critical skills gaps necessary to achieve the objectives of the national defense strategies required by and the national security strategies required by section 108 of the National Security Act of 1947 () by preparing students selected for the Defense Civilian Training Corps for Department of Defense careers relating to acquisition, digital technologies, critical technologies, science, engineering, finance, and other civilian occupations determined by the Secretary of Defense.section 113(g) of this title50 U.S.C. 3043

children.heading: .—Use of Resources and Programs
children.label: c .—Use of Resources and Programs
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment may leverage the resources and programs of the acquisition research organization within a civilian college or university that is described under (commonly referred to as the “Acquisition Innovation Research Center”) to carry out the requirements of this chapter.section 4142(a) of this title
